mnemonics.title: Remember 17 SDGs in Order
mnemonics.content: P-Poor (1), H-Hungry (2), H-Healthy (3), Q-Qualified (4), G-Gender (5), W-Water (6), E-Energy (7), W-Work (8), I-Industry (9), I-Inequalities (10), C-Cities (11), C-Consumption (12), C-Climate (13), O-Ocean (14), L-Land (15), P-Peace (16), P-Partnerships (17)

mnemonics.title: PEOPLE PLANET PROSPERITY PEACE
mnemonics.content: People (Goals 1-5), Planet (Goals 6,12-15), Prosperity (Goals 7-9,11), Peace (Goals 16-17) - The 4 P's framework

acronyms.usage: The 17 global goals adopted by UN in 2015 for 2030
The international body that adopted the SDGs
Business contribution to SDGs through ethical practices

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a flower with 17 petals, each petal representing a goal - they all connect at the center representing sustainable development
Think of a house with 17 rooms - every country has all 17 rooms to fill, unlike MDGs where some rooms were missing in developed countries
Use a spider web diagram - pulling one strand affects all others, showing how SDG 1 connects to SDG 7 (poverty affects energy access)

analogies.analogy: If MDGs were a primary school exam, SDGs are like a comprehensive university final - more subjects, harder questions, and everyone must take it
Think of SDGs like a human body - if one organ (goal) fails, it affects the entire system (all other goals)
Like a flight to a destination with a 15-year journey - we took off in 2015 and need to land by 2030
